rulings.Case Title: HEIDI DENTON et al VS. KWAN SHIU YAN FUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Inspection
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2015, Line 8, DEFENDANT KWUN FUNG'S Motion To Compel Inspection. Pro Tem Judge Sigrid Irias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org, and sigrid.irias@iriaslaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

Continued to October 9, 2015, to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. rulings.Case Title: HONGYANG AI VS. JACKY HONG LUI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories Against Plaintiff Hongyang Ai And His Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2015, Line 3, CROSS COMPLAINANT JACKY LUI'S Motion Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories Against Plaintiff Hongyang Ai And His Attorney. Pro Tem Judge Sigrid Irias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel responses to form interrogatories granted; responses to the form interrogatories to be served no later than ten days after service of notice of the court's written order. Sanctions awarded in favor of defendant and against plaintiff in the amount of $443.80, to be paid within ten days of service of notice of the court's written order. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org, and sigrid.irias@iriaslaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: THINK TECH LABS VS. MCLABS LLC DBA APTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, October 2, 2015, Line 5, DEFENDANT MCLABS LLC DBA APTO, DAVID MCGRAW Motion To Compel. The recommendation of the pro tem judge is adopted in full, which is: Defendant's motion to compel further responses is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff must provide a further code-compliant response to form interrogatory 9.2 within ten days and in all other respects the motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. The Court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: HANNAH MORANDO VS. EMIL DELRIO WHALEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 2, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF HANNAH ORANDO'S Motion To Compel Discovery And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Sigrid Irias, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to compel granted as to Form Interrogatory No. 2.8. Motion to compel granted as to Special Interrogatory No. 11. Motion to compel denied as to Special Interrogatory Nos. 12 and 13. Motion to compel denied as to Request for Production No. 11, granted as to Request for Production No. 12, denied as to Request for Production No. 13, granted as to Request for Production No. 14. Hearing required with respect to the motion to have responses to requests for admission deemed admitted and with respect to cross-requests for sanctions. Any party who contests this tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org, and sigrid.irias@iriaslaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm the court day prior to the hearing stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem Judge hearing the motion and the Pro Tem Judge will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare an order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
